notes: This manifest is the source of truth for candidate selection, internal review state, and approved UI attachment.
Only entries with ui_attachment.approved=true should render in the user-facing UI.

posts.scene_context: weekday office elevator mirror before a morning meeting
posts.style_concern: the trousers may break too sharply above loafers and make the outfit feel shorter
posts.intended_tone: believable office outfit, understated commute styling
posts.review_notes: Approved after regeneration. Elevator mirror framing, phone-obscured face, and visible hem/loafer relationship match the proportion-check policy.
posts.prompt: Create one realistic outfit preview image for a Korean mobile-first fashion community post. This is a styling preview, not an exact virtual try-on and not official product photography. Scene context: weekday office elevator mirror before a morning meeting. Style concern: the trousers may break too sharply above loafers and make the outfit feel shorter. Intended tone: believable office outfit, understated commute styling. Keep the image believable for a real Korean everyday social-fashion upload. Prefer half-body or full-body vertical mobile framing with a candid mirror selfie, hallway check, elevator mirror, apartment entryway, office mirror, or plain daily-life backdrop. If the scene is outdoors, keep it to an ordinary Seoul sidewalk or office-adjacent corner without scenic or campaign styling. Prefer a phone-covered face, partially cropped face, or lightly obscured identity over a polished face-forward beauty shot. Use ordinary indoor or overcast daylight and visible everyday imperfections such as slight grain, faint mirror smudges, or natural garment wrinkles. Make the outfit feel like weekday office, commute, lunch-plan, or after-work styling rather than an editorial campaign. Show the exact debate point clearly in frame so the viewer can judge proportion, fit, balance, or awkwardness from the image itself. Use the reference product images only as guidance for silhouette, category, and color family. Treat product references as secondary guidance only, not as the main subject of the image. Do not add any text overlay, collage layout, luxury-campaign polish, dramatic pose, cinematic angle, or catalog symmetry. Do not imply exact brand-official proof, real ownership history, durability proof, or exact garment transfer.

generation.revised_prompt:
Generate a single realistic, vertical 9:16 mobile photo: a candid weekday office elevator mirror selfie before a morning meeting in Seoul. Brushed-metal elevator interior, simple button panel and faint floor-indicator reflection. Ordinary cool-white fluorescent lighting with a touch of natural light spill from the hallway. Add believable imperfections: gentle grain, soft shadowing, faint smudges on the mirror, and natural garment wrinkles.

Subject: an office-going young adult taking the mirror selfie with one hand, phone covering most of the face for privacy; head slightly cropped at the top. Neutral, everyday styling—no glam makeup, no editorial polish.

Outfit (understated commute look):
- Outer layer: camel-tan wool zip-front jacket with a slim belt tied casually at the waist, slightly oversized sleeves; subtle texture, no visible logos.
- Underneath: cream ribbed turtleneck peeking at the collar and cuffs.
- Bottoms: navy, wide-leg trousers in a clean twill. IMPORTANT: show the styling concern clearly—at the shoe line the trouser hem creates a sharp break and crease right above the loafers, visually shortening the leg line. Capture this detail crisply in the frame.
- Shoes: black leather penny loafers with thin socks in a dark tone.

Framing and pose: half-to-full body vertical crop so the entire lower leg and shoes are visible in the mirror. Slightly angled stance with one foot turned a bit to the side so the trouser break over the loafer is obvious from both front and side. The phone blocks most of the face; hair and ear partially visible. A simple black tote or laptop bag on the shoulder is fine, but keep it subtle.

Mood: believable Korean everyday social-fashion upload—weekday office/commute tone. No text overlays, no collage, no dramatic lighting, no cinematic angles. Keep colors natural and slightly muted. Ensure the key debate point—the sharp trouser break above the loafers—is easy to judge from the image itself.

generation.revised_prompt:
Create one photorealistic, mobile-first vertical (9:16) outfit preview for a Korean social-fashion post. Candid elevator mirror selfie in a modest Seoul office building on a weekday morning. Stainless elevator walls, soft fluorescent lighting with a hint of overcast daylight from the lobby, faint mirror smudges and slight film grain. No text, no collage, no branding, not a campaign shot.

Subject: a young woman taking the selfie with her phone fully covering her face; hair in a low ponytail. Full body visible, natural stance with one foot slightly forward so proportions and hem length are clear.

Outfit styling (inspired by references for silhouette/color only, not exact products):
- Light sky-blue tweed short-sleeve mini dress with subtle texture, clean round neckline, tiny faux chest-pocket seams, and a slim self belt; A-line skirt hits mid-thigh with mild natural wrinkles near the waist.
- Over it, a dark chocolate-brown, collarless, lightly structured cropped jacket with long sleeves, hidden placket, gentle shoulder volume; worn open to soften the formality.
- Black leather loafers with thin black socks.
- Simple small off-white shoulder bag worn casually, or a beige canvas tote tucked under arm to suggest commuting (choose one bag, understated, no logos). Minimal jewelry.

Mood and intent: weekday outside-appointment office look that risks reading too formal for a normal morning; the brown cropped jacket + loafers + practical bag should dress it down. Keep everything believable for a real Korean daily post—ordinary lighting, slight imperfections, natural mirror perspective. Single image only; show the full silhouette clearly so viewers can judge the balance and whether the look still feels too formal.

generation.revised_prompt:
Create a single realistic vertical 9:16 outfit preview image for a Korean mobile-first social fashion post. Scene: weekday morning, candid elevator mirror selfie in a typical Seoul apartment building. Stainless walls with a handrail, faint fingerprints and a couple of small, slightly out-of-focus Korean info stickers on the side panel. Neutral fluorescent elevator lighting mixed with soft overcast daylight spilling in from the hallway. Slight image grain; believable phone camera look.

Person: identity obscured—phone covers most of the face; head slightly cropped at the top. Natural, everyday posture, one shoulder slightly forward as if rushing to work. No dramatic posing, no beauty-retouch polish.

Outfit concept (all-black core with one balancing accessory):
- Top: charcoal/dark heather V‑neck knit sweater inspired by the reference (guidance only), soft drape, ribbed hem and cuffs, sits at high hip. Slight front tuck to show waistline.
- Bottom: black tailored straight-leg trousers with a clean crease; proportion visible from hip to ankle. Subtle natural wrinkles at the knee from movement.
- Shoes: black leather loafers appropriate for office commute.
- Bag: structured black boxy leather shoulder/hand tote inspired by the reference (guidance only), carried by the handle and resting against the thigh. No readable logos.
- The debate-point accessory: a single muted blue‑gray silk scarf tied in a small knot around one bag handle, clearly visible to add balance and interest to the otherwise all‑black look.

Framing: half- to full-body in the elevator mirror so viewers can judge proportions—sweater length vs. trousers, bag size relative to torso, and the scarf accent pop. The phone is held in the right hand with a simple clear case; left hand holds the bag. Include faint mirror smudges and slight garment wrinkles for realism.

Styling/atmosphere: believable, everyday commute vibe—not a product catalog or exact virtual try-on. No text overlays, no collage, no dramatic lighting, no centered catalog symmetry, no brand claims. Keep it looking like a real Korean everyday social-fashion upload.

generation.revised_prompt:
Create one photo-realistic vertical 9:16 mirror selfie suitable for a Korean mobile-first fashion community post. Scene: weekday, just arrived at the office, taken in an elevator lobby mirror in Seoul. Lighting: cool, ordinary fluorescent mixed with soft daylight from a nearby window; keep it slightly flat and realistic. Add subtle grain and a few faint mirror smudges; no dramatic effects.

Framing: half-to-full body. Phone covers most of the face and the top of the head is slightly cropped. Relaxed pose, one hand in the pants pocket; a simple canvas tote strap rests on one shoulder. No visible logos or brand marks.

Outfit focus and debate point: show clearly how an oversized upper half may make the body look heavier than expected. Style the subject in a boxy, oversized navy blazer with lightly padded shoulders and sleeves that reach the knuckles, buttoned once over a thin white tee. Pair with camel-beige, high-waisted, pleated wide-leg trousers inspired by the reference (use only for guidance): extra-long with a soft break that slightly pools over black square-toe mules. Capture the proportions so the broad shoulder line, long blazer covering the hips, and wide trousers are all visible, with the waist mostly obscured. Include natural garment wrinkles and realistic drape.

Background details: simple Korean office hallway/elevator area—gray tiles, stainless elevator doors, a blurred notice board with indistinct papers, maybe an umbrella stand. Keep it mundane and believable, like a candid weekday check-in photo.

Aesthetic rules: realistic, everyday tone (not campaign-like), no text overlay, no collage, no cinematic angle, no perfect catalog symmetry. Treat the product reference as secondary guidance only and do not replicate exact brand details. Keep identity lightly obscured and make sure the proportions debate is easy to judge from the image.

generation.revised_prompt:

Narrow space with a tall mirror on a shoe cabinet, tiled floor, intercom panel and light switches on the wall, a couple pairs of slippers by the door, an umbrella stand, and a digital door lock visible. Natural indoor daylight with soft overhead light. Slight image grain and a few faint mirror smudges for realism.

Subject: a young adult standing straight, face mostly hidden by the phone; top of head slightly cropped to keep identity unobtrusive. Casual candid posture, one shoulder slightly relaxed.

generation.revised_prompt:
Create one realistic mobile vertical photo for a Korean fashion community post. Candid elevator mirror selfie in a typical Seoul office-apartment elevator: stainless steel walls, button panel and faint mirror smudges visible, cool fluorescent lighting, slight phone grain. The person’s face is obscured by their phone; framing is half-to-full body, natural stance, weekday after-work mood.

Outfit styling (use as guidance; do not show logos or brand-perfect product shots):
- Outer: dark charcoal tailored blazer, mixed-texture with ribbed knit sleeves, mid-hip length, slightly relaxed fit.
- Inner top: loose light-gray button-up shirt in linen-like fabric. Make it obviously longer and more voluminous than the blazer so it hangs 5–8 cm below the blazer hem. Wide spread collar sitting over the blazer lapels, top two buttons open with a thin black camisole just visible. Shirt cuffs extend past the knit sleeves by 2–3 cm. Include natural wrinkles.
- Bottoms: straight black trousers with light creases; simple black loafers. Small black shoulder bag and a slim belt. Subtle office ID clip peeking from a pocket to imply weekday evening plans.

Pose and composition to show the debate point clearly: the subject angles slightly, one hand holding the phone, the other gently pulling the blazer edge to reveal the long shirttail and extra cuff length, so viewers can judge the proportion and whether the inner top overpowers the jacket. Keep everything believable and everyday, not editorial: mild lint on knit sleeves, faint mirror smudges, natural garment wrinkles. No text overlay, no collage, no cinematic lighting. Maintain a casual Korean daily-fashion mood.

generation.revised_prompt:

Lighting is ordinary cool fluorescent; include slight grain and faint mirror smudges so it feels candid, not glossy. Background: beige curtain, narrow bench with a folded cardigan and a plain paper shopping bag, gray tile floor. No text or logos.

Subject: anonymous person with smartphone covering their face; upper part of face slightly cropped. Natural posture facing the mirror. 

Outfit styling (inspired by, but not an exact depiction of, a generic black blazer):
- Black two-button blazer with lightly structured shoulders and crisp notch lapels; mid-hip length; slightly boxy but tailored; sleeves a touch long. Top button fastened, bottom unbuttoned so shape is clear.
- White cotton crewneck T-shirt underneath.
- Straight light-gray trousers with a clean break.
- Clean white sneakers.
- Thin black belt, simple silver watch, soft beige canvas tote on shoulder. No visible brand marks.

Details to emphasize the debate point (whether the blazer feels too sharp/expensive for daily rotation): make the shoulder line and lapels look slightly sharp, show the hem hitting mid-hip, and show sleeve length and overall proportion clearly from head to shoes. Include subtle garment wrinkles at the elbows and waist and a tiny, unreadable hangtag peeking from inside the sleeve to suggest an in-store try-on. Keep the overall tone believable for an everyday Korean social-fashion upload; avoid editorial polish, dramatic angles, collage, or catalog symmetry.

generation.revised_prompt: Create one realistic mobile-first outfit preview photo for a Korean social-fashion community size-help post. Vertical 9:16 framing. Scene: candid apartment elevator mirror selfie in Seoul—stainless steel walls, button panel and handrail visible, cool fluorescent light, faint mirror smudges, slightly grainy phone image. No other people, no readable signage. Subject: adult of average build; face partially cropped and mostly covered by the phone. Weekday office vibe. Outfit: charcoal lightweight long-sleeve tee tucked into high-rise black wide-leg pleated trousers with a slim black belt; simple black leather loafers. Emphasize the pants fit at the hips: three-quarter stance with one leg slightly forward; one hand lightly pinching the waistband to show 1–2 cm ease; subtle pulling lines across the upper hip/pocket area to question whether the hip ease is enough; clean drape through the thigh into a gently pooling hem so viewers can judge overall silhouette and length. Natural wrinkles and texture, believable lighting, everyday imperfections. No text overlays, no collage, no brand logos, not an exact virtual try-on or product photo—just a realistic community styling preview image that clearly shows hip ease and silhouette.

generation.revised_prompt:
Create one realistic, mobile-first vertical outfit preview image (9:16) for a Korean everyday social-fashion post. Scene: candid elevator mirror selfie in a Seoul apartment/office building elevator with brushed metal walls and soft overhead fluorescent lights. Light smudges on the mirror and slight smartphone grain; no text, no collage, no cinematic angles.

Subject: an adult taking a mirror selfie with phone covering the face; identity lightly obscured and upper head slightly cropped. Natural stance, shoulders square to the mirror so the shoulder line is clearly visible.

Outfit focus: a gray, drop-shoulder, slightly oversized woven shirt (linen-blend look) with subtle texture, minimal buttons, and one chest pocket. Shoulder seams fall visibly past the natural shoulder to highlight the volume debate. Sleeves worn two different ways to show proportion: one sleeve unrolled and long, the other casually rolled once to the forearm. Shirt is lightly half-tucked at the front into tailored black high-waisted straight trousers; back and one side left untucked so the drape and width are obvious. Add a thin black leather belt, simple black loafers, and a small neutral crossbody or tote hanging from one shoulder. Include natural garment wrinkles and a gentle fold at the upper arm so viewers can judge bulk.

Styling vibe: weekday office/commute—practical and believable, not editorial. No visible brand logos. Keep colors neutral (gray shirt, black trousers, black shoes) so attention stays on shoulder volume and proportion. Lighting feels ordinary indoor or overcast daylight filtering in from elevator gap; shadows remain soft. Frame half-to-full body vertically, ensuring the shoulders, sleeve length, and torso balance are fully in view. Maintain an authentic, everyday Korean upload feel.

generation.revised_prompt:
Create one realistic, vertical 9:16 mobile photo for a Korean fashion community post. Scene: candid mirror selfie in a typical Seoul office elevator—brushed steel walls, button panel to the side, faint fingerprints and smudges on the mirror, cool fluorescent lighting with a touch of daylight spill. Add slight phone-camera grain. 

Subject: everyday Korean office worker (gender-neutral), head slightly cropped and face fully covered by a black smartphone. Natural stance, not a fashion pose. 

Outfit: weekday office style. Pale blue oxford shirt tucked into taupe-beige pleated trousers inspired by the reference (use as guidance only—no exact product copy or branding). Slim black leather belt. Black leather penny loafers. Add light, believable garment wrinkles and creases.

Important styling debate shown clearly: trouser width and shoe break comparison within one single frame. Do this by making the left pant leg temporarily clipped at the back hem/side to create a straighter, trimmer line that finishes just at the top of the loafer with a small break; the right pant leg remains wider and slightly longer with a deeper break and a soft pool over the loafer. Include a tiny silver binder clip visible at the back of the left hem to hint at the temporary adjustment. Keep both shoes fully in frame and centered so viewers can judge proportion and balance.

Avoid any text overlay, collage layout, or brand logos. Keep the tone believable and unpolished: slight mirror dust, soft reflections, natural elevator environment. Make sure the trousers’ color and silhouette echo the provided beige wide-leg reference only as a general guide.

generation.revised_prompt:
Create a single realistic vertical 9:16 smartphone photo for a Korean mobile-first fashion community post. Scene: a cold weekday office commute elevator mirror selfie in a typical Seoul office building. Anonymous subject with phone covering most of the face; no eye contact, identity subtly obscured. Lighting is cool fluorescent elevator lighting with a touch of overcast daylight feel. Include slight grain, faint mirror smudges, and natural garment wrinkles for believability—no glossy finish.

Wardrobe and debate shown clearly in one frame (not a collage):
- On body: a camel wool zip-front coat with a soft stand collar and simple tie belt (use the reference only for silhouette and color family). This version is true-to-size—zipped to mid-chest and lightly tied. Shoulder seams align right at the shoulder point; sleeves are modestly roomy; torso looks balanced.
- In the other hand: the same camel coat in an oversized size, held on a hanger at shoulder height directly next to the body so viewers can compare width and sleeve volume. The oversized coat’s dropped shoulders extend past the subject’s natural shoulder line, body is boxier, and sleeves appear balloonier, clearly showing how it could make the upper body look broad if chosen incorrectly.

Base outfit under the coats: cream ribbed turtleneck knit, straight black trousers, black leather ankle boots. A slim black tote or crossbody is on the shoulder with a gray wool scarf peeking out and a small transit card clipped to the strap. Subtle weekday commute vibe—no logos or brand identifiers.

Framing: half- to three-quarter-body vertical composition emphasizing shoulders, chest, and hips for proportion checking. Background: ordinary stainless-steel elevator interior with button panel and floor indicator slightly blurred; no readable text. Keep everything looking like a real Korean everyday social-fashion upload: casual posture, slight mirror smudges, soft shadows, understated and practical winter office style. No text overlay, no collage layout, no dramatic angles, no studio polish.
